items.MBTI-003: You are usually more persuaded by ideas that resonate emotionally than by factual arguments.
items.MBTI-005: You can usually stay calm, even under a lot of pressure.
items.MBTI-006: You find building relationships with strangers or promoting yourself very intimidating.
items.MBTI-007: You can prioritize and plan tasks effectively, usually finishing smoothly before deadlines.
items.MBTI-008: People's stories and emotions are more convincing to you than numbers or data.
items.MBTI-009: You like using organizing tools such as schedules and checklists.
items.MBTI-010: Even a small mistake can make you question your overall ability and knowledge.
items.MBTI-011: You feel comfortable walking up to someone you find interesting and starting a conversation.
items.MBTI-013: When deciding what to do, you value facts more than people's feelings.
items.MBTI-014: You often let the day unfold naturally without any plan at all.
items.MBTI-016: You enjoy taking part in group activities.
items.MBTI-019: You actively seek out new experiences and fields of knowledge to explore.
items.MBTI-020: You tend to worry that things will get worse.
items.MBTI-021: You prefer hobbies or activities you can do alone rather than in groups.
items.MBTI-022: You cannot imagine making a living by writing fiction.
items.MBTI-023: You focus more on efficiency when making decisions, even if that means overlooking some emotional factors.
items.MBTI-024: You tend to finish chores before relaxing.
items.MBTI-026: In social situations, you usually wait for others to introduce themselves first.
items.MBTI-028: You are not easily influenced by emotional arguments.
items.MBTI-032: You become bored or lose interest when a discussion becomes very theoretical.
items.MBTI-034: You find it hard to keep a regular work or study schedule.
items.MBTI-035: You rarely doubt the decisions you have made.
items.MBTI-036: Your friends would say you are lively and outgoing.
items.MBTI-042: You enjoy exploring unfamiliar ideas and perspectives.
items.MBTI-043: You can easily connect with people you have just met.
items.MBTI-045: You are still bothered by mistakes you made a long time ago.
items.MBTI-046: You are not very interested in discussing theories about what the future world might look like.
items.MBTI-048: When making decisions, you focus more on how affected people feel than on what is most logical or efficient.
items.MBTI-054: If you feel a decision is right, you usually act on it directly without needing more evidence.
items.MBTI-060: You trust that things will work out in your favor.
items.MBTI-061: At parties, you take the initiative to talk with different people instead of staying in one corner.
items.MBTI-062: After being alone for a long time, you noticeably feel your energy drop.
items.MBTI-064: When you hear good news, you cannot help sharing it with many people right away.
items.MBTI-066: In unfamiliar environments, you prefer to observe for a while before speaking.
items.MBTI-067: Others would describe you as someone who talks quite a lot.
items.MBTI-071: You prefer a lively open office area to a closed private office.
items.MBTI-073: At large events, you often feel somewhat tired from being surrounded by crowds.
items.MBTI-074: You feel clearly uncomfortable when people suddenly push you to speak in front of others.
items.MBTI-076: After social events, you often replay what you said and wonder whether anything was inappropriate.
items.MBTI-079: When facing a new idea, you first think about how it could be implemented in reality.
items.MBTI-080: You can be pulled out of an experience by one unreasonable detail, even if the overall work is good.
items.MBTI-081: You prefer imagining life ten years from now to recalling things that happened in the past.
items.MBTI-082: When making plans, you leave room for new opportunities that may appear in the future.
items.MBTI-083: When you see an ordinary object, you easily think of many different uses or metaphors for it.
items.MBTI-085: Compared with a long-term blueprint, you care more about what specifically needs to be done today.
items.MBTI-086: You often suddenly think of 'what if' possibilities.
items.MBTI-087: When someone gives only a general direction, you ask for more details before starting.
items.MBTI-088: You like discussing hypothetical situations and possibilities with others.
items.MBTI-090: When watching movies, you focus more on the theme of the story than on whether the scenes are realistic.
items.MBTI-091: You trust what you can see and touch more than abstract theories.
items.MBTI-092: For you, exceptions and special cases are often more interesting than the rules themselves.
items.MBTI-093: When you need to give negative feedback, you try to say it gently so you do not hurt the other person's feelings.
items.MBTI-095: When a friend confides in you, you usually start by helping them analyze the problem rather than simply keeping them company.
items.MBTI-096: For you, 'focus on the issue, not the person' is a very important principle.
items.MBTI-097: Even when you know the truth will make someone uncomfortable, you still think it should be stated honestly.
items.MBTI-098: You easily notice subtle changes in other people's emotions.
items.MBTI-100: In team discussions, you actively point out flaws in other people's arguments.
items.MBTI-101: When you reject someone, you feel guilty for a long time if they seem disappointed.
items.MBTI-103: When someone is emotional, you often do not really know what to say to comfort them.
items.MBTI-105: You would rather sacrifice some efficiency if it helps the team atmosphere stay harmonious.
items.MBTI-106: You actively avoid topics that might lead to arguments, even when you disagree.
items.MBTI-107: When arguing with someone, you care more about whether the logic is rigorous than whether the tone sounds pleasant.
items.MBTI-108: When you see something unfair, you find it hard to stay silent.
items.MBTI-111: If your plan for the day is disrupted, you feel somewhat irritated.
items.MBTI-112: When facing a large task, you first break the steps down in writing before starting.
items.MBTI-113: You prefer flexible, open plans to schedules arranged down to the hour.
items.MBTI-114: You often adjust priorities by feel while doing things instead of strictly following the original plan.
items.MBTI-115: Your desk is often full of things in progress and is rarely completely tidy.
items.MBTI-116: When starting a project, you tend to begin casually and organize the details along the way.
items.MBTI-117: You prepare a fixed weekly routine and try to follow it.
items.MBTI-120: You often put simple tasks off until late because you hesitate over whether to start now.
items.MBTI-121: You regularly review your goals and progress to make sure you are moving in the intended direction.
items.MBTI-122: When working with others, you tend to take on the role of arranging and coordinating things.
items.MBTI-124: When you suddenly think of a more interesting idea, you immediately set aside the task at hand to try it.
items.MBTI-125: After finishing work, you systematically organize files and records so they are easy to find later.
items.MBTI-126: For you, delivering on time is more important than making a few more changes at the last moment.
items.MBTI-128: Once you make a mistake, you replay the scene repeatedly in your mind.
items.MBTI-129: When you receive praise, you wonder whether the other person is exaggerating.
items.MBTI-130: In unexpected situations, you can quickly calm down, assess first, and then act.
items.MBTI-132: Before going out, you repeatedly check whether you have brought important items.
items.MBTI-133: When facing a difficult task, you usually believe you can find a way to solve it.
items.MBTI-135: When plans cannot keep up with changes, you still trust that you can handle it.
items.MBTI-136: When under pressure, you tend to blame yourself first rather than the environment or other people.
items.MBTI-138: After embarrassing yourself in front of others, you think, 'I will do better next time,' rather than regretting it for a long time.
items.MBTI-140: When others reject or criticize you, you easily magnify it into 'Am I completely useless?'
items.MBTI-142: When others need you, you worry that you may not be able to help.
